channel.title: Readings on MIT OpenCourseWare
channel.description: Recent content in Readings on MIT OpenCourseWare

item.title: 1. Vectors and Matrices
item.description: Supplementary exercises on vectors and matrices.

item.title: 1. Vectors and Matrices
item.description: Solutions to supplementary exercises on vectors and matrices.

item.title: 18.02 Supplementary Notes and Problems
item.description:
These notes and exercises were written by Prof. Arthur Mattuck and are designed to supplement the textbook.

Part I: Notes

SECTIONS TOPICS
D Determinants (PDF)
M Matrices and linear algebra (PDF)
K Kepler’s second law (PDF)
TA The tangent approximation (PDF)
SD Second derivative test (PDF)
LS Least squares interpolation (PDF)
N Non-independent variables (PDF)
P Partial differential equations (PDF)
I Limits in iterated integrals (PDF)

item.title: 2. Partial Differentiation
item.description: Solutions to supplementary exercises on partial differentiation, functions and partial derivatives, the tangent plane, linear approximation, differentials, approximations, gradient and directional derivative, the chain rule, maximum-minimum problems, least-squares interpolation, 2nd derivative criterion, boundary curves, Lagrange multipliers, non-independent variables, and partial differential equations.

item.title: 2. Partial Differentiation
item.description: Supplementary exercises on partial differentiation, functions and partial derivatives, the tangent plane, linear approximation, differentials, approximations, gradient and directional derivative, the chain rule, maximum-minimum problems, least-squares interpolation, 2nd derivative criterion, boundary curves, Lagrange multipliers, non-independent variables, and partial differential equations.

item.title: 3. Double Integrals
item.description: Solutions to supplementary exercises on double integrals.

item.title: 3. Double Integrals
item.description: Supplementary exercises on double integrals in rectangular and polar coordinates, applications of double integration, and changing variables in multiple integrals.

item.title: 4. Line Integrals in the Plane
item.description: Solutions to supplementary exercises on line integrals in the plane, plane vector fields, gradient fields and exact differentials, Green’s theorem, two-dimensional flux, simply-connected regions, and multiply-connected regions.

item.title: 4. Line Integrals in the Plane
item.description: Supplementary exercises on line integrals in the plane, plane vector fields, gradient fields and exact differentials, Green’s theorem, two-dimensional flux, simply-connected regions, and multiply-connected regions.

item.title: 5. Triple Integrals
item.description: Solutions to supplementary exercises on triple integrals in rectangular, cylindrical, and spherical coordinates, and gravitational attraction.

item.title: 5. Triple Integrals
item.description: Supplementary exercises on triple integrals in rectangular, cylindrical, and spherical coordinates, and gravitational attraction.

item.title: 6. Vector Integral Calculus in Space
item.description: Solutions to supplementary exercises on vector integral calculus in space.

item.title: 6. Vector Integral Calculus in Space
item.description: Supplementary exercises on vector integral calculus in space.

item.title: CV. Changing Variables in Multiple Integrals
item.description: Supplementary notes on changing variables in multiple integrals, putting in the limits, and changing coordinates in triple integrals.

item.title: D. Determinants
item.description: Supplementary notes on determinants, Laplace expansion by cofactors, and area and volume interpretation of the determinant.

item.title: G. Gravitational Attraction
item.description: Supplementary notes on gravitational attraction.

item.title: I. Limits in Iterated Integrals
item.description: Supplementary notes on limits in iterated integrals, double integrals in rectangular coordinates, double integrals in polar coordinates, triple integrals in rectangular and cylindrical coordinates, and spherical coordinates.

item.title: K. Kepler's Second Law
item.description: Supplementary notes on differentiation of products of vectors, Kepler’s second law, and the central force.

item.title: LS. Least Squares Interpolation
item.description: Supplementary notes on least squares interpolation, the least-squares line, and fitting curves by least squares.

item.title: M. Matrices and Linear Algebra
item.description: Supplementary notes on matrices and linear algebra.

item.title: Matlab Instructions
item.description: Instructions on how to use the program MATLAB, which students must use on some problem sets.

item.title: N. Non-independent Variables
item.description: Supplementary notes on partial differentiation with non-independent variables, differentials versus chain rule, abstract partial differentiation, rules relating partial derivatives, and changing the independent variables.

item.title: P. Partial Differential Equations
item.description: Supplementary notes on partial differential equations.

item.title: Readings
item.description:
Listed in the table below are reading assignments for each lecture session.

“Text” refers to the course textbook: Edwards, Henry C., and David E. Penney. Multivariable Calculus. 6th ed. Lebanon, IN: Prentice Hall, 2002. ISBN: 9780130339676.

“Notes” refers to the “18.02 Supplementary Notes and Problems” written by Prof. Arthur Mattuck.

LEC # TOPICS READINGS
I. Vectors and matrices
0 Vectors Text: Section 12.1
1 Dot product Text: Section 12.2

item.title: SD. Second Derivative Test
item.description: Supplementary notes on the second derivative test.

item.title: TA. The Tangent Approximation
item.description: Supplementary notes on the tangent approximation, partial derivatives, the tangent plane, the approximation formula, and critique of the approximation formula.

item.title: V1. Plane Vector Fields
item.description: Supplementary notes on vector integral calculus, vector fields in the plane, gradient fields, flow fields, and velocity fields,

item.title: V10. The Divergence Theorem
item.description: Supplementary notes on the divergence theorem.

item.title: V11. Line Integrals in Space
item.description: Supplementary notes on line integrals in space, curves in space, gradient fields, and path-independence.

item.title: V12. Gradient Fields in Space
item.description: Supplementary notes on gradient fields in space, the criterion for gradient fields, the curl in space, and exact differentials.

item.title: V13. Stokes' Theorem
item.description: Supplementary notes on Stokes’ theorem, the relation of Stokes’ theorem to Green’s theorem, and proof of Stokes’ theorem.

item.title: V14. Some Topological Questions
item.description: Supplementary notes on vector integral calculus, Stokes’ theorem, and topological questions.

item.title: V15. Relation to Physics
item.description: Supplementary notes on the use of the divergence theorem, Stokes’ theorem in space,and Green’s theorem in the plane in electromagnetic theory.

item.title: V2. Gradient Fields and Exact Differentials
item.description: Supplementary notes on gradient fields and exact differentials, criterion for gradient fields, and finding the potential function.

item.title: V3. Two-dimensional Flux
item.description: Supplementary notes on two-dimensional flux.

item.title: V4. Green's Theorem in Normal Form
item.description: Supplementary notes on Green’s theorem in normal form, Green’s theorem for flux, the two-dimensional divergence, and an interpretation for curl F.

item.title: V5. Simply-Connected Regions
item.description: Supplementary notes on simply-connected regions, the extended Green’s theorem, multiply-connected regions, and the Curl theorem.

item.title: V6. Multiply-connected Regions; Topology
item.description: Supplementary notes on multiply-connected regions and topology.

item.title: V7. Laplace's Equation and Harmonic Functions
item.description: Supplementary notes on Laplace’s equation, harmonic functions, vector fields, boundary-value problems, existence and uniqueness for harmonic functions, and the uniqueness theorem.

item.title: V8. Vector Fields in Space
item.description: Supplementary notes on vector fields in space.

item.title: V9. Surface Integrals
item.description: Supplementary notes on surface integrals, the surface integral for flux, flux through general surfaces, and other types of surface integrals.
